records.ety: [AS. unfealdan. See 1st Un-, and Fold, v. t.]

defs: To open the folds of; to expand; to spread out; as, to unfold a tablecloth.
quotes: Unfold thy forehead gathered into frowns.

defs: To open, as anything covered or close; to lay open to view or contemplation; to bring out in all the details, or by successive development; to display; to disclose; to reveal; to elucidate; to explain; as, to unfold one's designs; to unfold the principles of a science.
quotes: Unfold the passion of my love.

defs: To release from a fold or pen; as, to unfold sheep.
To open; to expand; to become disclosed or developed.
quotes: The wind blows cold While the morning doth unfold.
